raw_text: I get satisfaction from helping others and if this were taken away from me I would get depressed
People often call me a sulker
I'm able to keep a problem out of my mind until I have time to deal with it
I work out my anxiety through doing something constructive and creative like painting or woodwork
I often change my opinion about people; at one time I think highly of them, at another time I think they're worthless
I am able to find good reasons for everything I do
I'm able to laugh at myself pretty easily
People tend to mistreat me
If someone mugged me and stole my money, I'd rather he'd be helped than punished
If I have a conflict with someone, I try to think of what might have been my part in it
People say I tend to ignore unpleasant facts as if they didn't exist
I often feel superior to people I'm with
Someone is robbing me emotionally of all I've got
When there's real danger, it's as if I'm not there and I feel no fear
If I'm treated unfairly, I stand up for my rights
I manage danger as if I were Superman
I pride myself on my ability to cut people down to size
I often act impulsively when something is bothering me
Actually I'm pretty worthless
When dealing with people they often end up feeling what I feel
I get more satisfaction from my fantasies than from my real life
I withdraw when I'm angry
When I'm in difficulties I often feel unreal
I've got special talents that allow me to go through life with no problems
I prefer to talk about abstract things rather than about my feelings
There are always good reasons when things don't work out for me
I work more things out in my daydreams than in my real life
When people get angry with me, I tend to think they are exaggerating
Sometimes I think I'm an angel and other times I think I'm a devil
If someone gets angry at me I tend to get annoyed by things I usually ignore
I get openly aggressive when I feel hurt
I hardly remember anything from my early school years
I withdraw when I'm sad
I always feel that someone I know is like a guardian angel
I'm actually worse than people think I am
As far as I'm concerned, people are either good or bad
If my boss bugged me, I might make a mistake in my work or work more slowly so as to get back at him
There is someone I know who can do anything and who is absolutely fair and just
If I've experienced something unpleasant then the next day I've sometimes forgotten what it was about
Helping others makes me feel good
I can keep the lid on my feelings if letting them out would interfere with what I'm doing
I'm usually able to see the funny side of an otherwise painful predicament
I often find myself being very nice to people who by all rights I should be angry at
There's no such thing as 'finding a little good in everyone,' if you're bad, you're all bad
When something I do doesn't turn out well, I try to find out what I may have overlooked
People tend to be dishonest with me
When I have to face a difficult situation I try to imagine what it will be like and plan ways to cope with it
Doctors never really understand what is wrong with me
After I fight for my rights, I tend to apologize for my assertiveness
If someone is annoying me, then I tell them without hurting their feelings
I'm often told that I don't show my feelings
When I feel bad, I try to be with someone
If I can predict that I'm going to be sad ahead of time, I can cope better
No matter how much I complain, I never get a satisfactory response
Instead of saying exactly what I feel, I explain my thoughts extensively
Often I find that I don't feel anything when the situation would seem to warrant strong emotions
When I feel depressed or anxious, I like to engage in some creative or physical activity
If I got into a crisis, I would seek out someone to share my worries with
If I have an aggressive thought, I feel the need to do something to compensate for it
When something exciting is happening, I tend to fuss over unimportant details
